configurations.name: DD_AAS_DOTNET_EXTENSION_VERSION
configurations.description: Holds the running version of the Azure App Services Site Extension. This env var is set in the applicationHost.xdt file.

configurations.name: DD_ACTION_EXECUTION_ID
configurations.description: Sets the action execution ID reported as CI metadata for AWS CodePipeline builds. This value is used as the CI job identifier when running a CodeBuild job initiated by CodePipeline.

configurations.name: DD_AGENTLESS_LOG_SUBMISSION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables direct log submission to Datadog without routing logs through the Datadog Agent.

configurations.name: DD_AGENTLESS_LOG_SUBMISSION_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the intake URL used when agentless log submission is enabled.

configurations.name: DD_AGENT_HOST
configurations.description: The host name to use to connect the Datadog agent for traces. The host name can be IPv4, IPv6, or a domain name. If DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL is specified, the value of DD_AGENT_HOST is ignored.

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables AI Guard request inspection in the tracer; when disabled, AI Guard checks are skipped.

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: AI Guard: sets the base endpoint URL for the AI Guard REST API. If unset, defaults to `https://app.<DD_SITE>/api/v2/ai-guard` (the tracer appends `/evaluate`).

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_MAX_CONTENT_SIZE
configurations.description: Max size of the content property set in the meta-struct

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_MAX_MESSAGES_LENGTH
configurations.description: Maximum number of conversational messages allowed to be set in the meta-struct

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Timeout used in calls to the AI Guard REST API in milliseconds (default 5000)

configurations.name: DD_API_KEY
configurations.description: To use the Datadog CLI in Azure Cloud Shell, open a cloud shell, set your API key and site in the `DD_API_KEY` and `DD_SITE` environment variables, and use `npx` to run the CLI directly:

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_DOWNSTREAM_BODY_ANALYSIS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Defines the probability of a downstream request body being sampled, or said differently, defines the overall number of requests for which the request and response body should be sampled / analysed.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether API Security features are enabled. If unset, API Security is enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether API endpoint definitions are collected for API catalog features. If unset, endpoint collection is enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_MESSAGE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of API endpoints included in a single endpoint-collection telemetry message. If unset, a default limit is used.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_MAX_DOWNSTREAM_REQUEST_BODY_ANALYSIS
configurations.description: The maximum number of downstream requests per request for which the request and response body should be analysed.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_SAMPLE_DELAY
configurations.description: Sets the delay (in seconds) between API Security schema samples. This setting is intended for system testing and is not meant for general use.

configurations.name: DD_APM_FLUSH_DEADLINE_MILLISECONDS
configurations.description: Doesn't crash when spans are finished early and reached impending timeout

configurations.name: DD_APM_TRACING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables sending APM trace data. When disabled, tracing still runs to support other products, but only periodic heartbeat traces and manually kept traces are sent.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_AUTO_USER_INSTRUMENTATION_MODE
configurations.description: Controls the automated user tracking mode for user IDs and logins collections. Possible values: * 'anonymous': will hash user IDs and user logins before collecting them * 'anon': alias for 'anonymous' * 'safe': deprecated alias for 'anonymous' * 'identification': will collect user IDs and logins without redaction * 'ident': alias for 'identification' * 'extended': deprecated alias for 'identification' * 'disabled': will not collect user IDs and logins Unknown values will be considered as 'disabled'

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_COLLECT_ALL_HEADERS
configurations.description: When enabled, AppSec collects all request headers for security analysis instead of only the default allowlisted subset. Because header values can contain sensitive information, this flag should be used carefully; header redaction and obfuscation settings still apply. The header-collection rules (which headers are always collected vs collected only on certain events) remain in effect unless this flag forces collection of every header

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the AppSec product inside the tracer. When enabled the tracer initializes the AppSec module (in-app WAF, header/body collection and AppSec telemetry)

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_GRAPHQL_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_JSON
configurations.description: Path to a local JSON response template that replaces the default backend template when the tracer performs a block action for a GraphQL request. If unset or the file cannot be read the tracer falls back to the built-in default blocking template

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HEADER_COLLECTION_REDACTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: When enabled the tracer applies the AppSec header/value redaction policies to any header values it collects. Header redaction uses configured regexps and obfuscation rules to replace sensitive substrings with a stable redaction token; this flag prevents accidental leak of PII in headers collected for AppSec detection or telemetry

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_HTML
configurations.description: Path to a local HTML template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be HTML. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_JSON
configurations.description: Path to a local JSON template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be JSON. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_COLLECTED_HEADERS
configurations.description: Caps the number of headers the tracer will store/forward for AppSec purposes to avoid extremely wide span/tag vectors and large payloads. Headers beyond the configured cap are handled according to tracer policy (skipped or only name-collected). This limit protects performance and privacy; use the header collection docs for guidance on safe values.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACES
configurations.description: Limits how many separate stacktraces are captured and attached to an AppSec/IAST event to keep payload sizes reasonable. Evidence stacktraces are useful for triage, but unbounded capture can leak sensitive data and consume excessive bandwidth; this limit balances evidence value and safety. Set to 0 to collect all.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACE_DEPTH
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of stack frames retained for any captured stacktrace attached to AppSec events. Tracers should prioritise top-of-stack frames and truncate deeper frames when this limit is reached. The setting helps keep evidentiary stacktraces compact while preserving the most actionable frames

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_OBFUSCATION_PARAMETER_KEY_REGEXP
configurations.description: A regular expression used by the AppSec obfuscator to identify parameter keys that must cause full redaction. When any `key_path` string matches this regexp the entire parameter is replaced by `<redacted by datadog>`. The default key regexp matches common secret/key names (password, token, authorization, jwt, etc.) an empty string disables the key regexp

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_OBFUSCATION_PARAMETER_VALUE_REGEXP
configurations.description: Regular expression used to find and redact sensitive substrings inside parameter values and highlights. Matches are replaced with `<redacted by datadog>`. This regexp complements the key regexp: the key regexp takes precedence; when no key match exists, the value regexp runs. The default pattern targeted at tokens, JWTs, private key blocks, and other credential shapes. Empty string disables the value regexp

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_RASP_COLLECT_REQUEST_BODY
configurations.description: Controls whether the RASP (runtime exploit-prevention) collectors include the request body when performing detection or making block decisions. Request bodies can contain sensitive data and large payloads, so collection obeys size limits and obfuscation rules.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_RASP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Turns on the tracer's RASP / Exploit Prevention capabilities. When enabled the tracer runs synchronous, pre-execution checks and may generate block actions or exploit signals. RASP may have separate activation and implementation details per language; enabling it usually activates extra synchronous instrumentation and additional evidence capture for detections

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_RULES
configurations.description: Override the default rules file provided. Must be a path to a valid JSON rules file

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_SCA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the tracer's runtime Software Composition Analysis (Runtime SCA) capability, which reports runtime dependency/SBOM information and powers runtime vulnerability detection. Useful for testing and demonstrations as well as production runtime vulnerability workflows; SCA may have billing/usage implications and language compatibility constraints described in the SCA runbooks

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_STACK_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables security-related stack traces generation when security events occur.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_TRACE_RATE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Controls the maximum amount of AppSec traces, per second.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_WAF_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Limits the WAF synchronous execution time (in microseconds)

configurations.name: DD_APP_KEY
configurations.description: Sets the application key used to authenticate LLM Observability dataset and experiment requests in agentless mode. If agentless mode is enabled and this is not set, dataset/experiment operations that require an application key will fail.

configurations.name: DD_AZURE_APP_SERVICES
configurations.description: Configuration key which is used as a flag to tell us whether we are instrumenting an Azure App Service using the AAS Site Extension.

configurations.name: DD_AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP
configurations.description: Azure Resource Group name reported as cloud metadata for CI Visibility and telemetry correlation.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AGENTLESS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Agentless mode in CI Visibility. Default value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AGENTLESS_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the intake base URL used for agentless CI test reporting. When set, payloads are sent to this URL instead of the default site-based intake URL.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AUTO_INSTRUMENTATION_PROVIDER
configurations.description: Marks CI test sessions as running under an auto-instrumentation workflow. Any non-empty value enables this marker for telemetry.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_DANGEROUSLY_FORCE_COVERAGE
configurations.description: Force-enables CI Visibility code coverage collection for debugging, even when remote settings disable it.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_DANGEROUSLY_FORCE_TEST_SKIPPING
configurations.description: Force-enables test skipping logic for debugging, even when remote settings disable it.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_EARLY_FLAKE_DETECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Is disabled if DD_CIVISIBILITY_EARLY_FLAKE_DETECTION_ENABLED is false

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enabling or disabling CI Visibility

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_FLAKY_RETRY_COUNT
configurations.description: Can be set to any non-negative number to change the maximum number of retries per test case.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_FLAKY_RETRY_ENABLED
configurations.description: A kill switch for the auto test retries feature.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_GIT_UNSHALLOW_ENABLED
configurations.description: Provide users the option to enable/disable the `git unshallow` process wheen retrieving git metadata.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_GIT_UPLOAD_ENABLED
configurations.description: Disable git upload if the DD_CIVISIBILITY_GIT_UPLOAD_ENABLED is set to false

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_IMPACTED_TESTS_DETECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables CI Visibility impacted tests detection (TIA). When enabled, impacted-tests detection is requested/applied as part of execution settings propagated to child processes.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_ITR_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Intelligent Test Runner in CI Visibility. Default Value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_MANUAL_API_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables CI Visibility manual API mode for reporting test events programmatically without framework auto-instrumentation.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_RUM_FLUSH_WAIT_MILLIS
configurations.description: CI Visibility (Selenium): after calling `window.DD_RUM.stopSession()` at the end of a browser test, waits this many milliseconds before proceeding to allow RUM data to flush (default: 500ms).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_TEST_COMMAND
configurations.description: CI Visibility: sets the test command string propagated to child test JVMs (for example, `mvn test` or `gradle test`). It is used to build the CI Visibility test session name when an explicit session name is not set.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_TEST_MODULE_ID
configurations.description: CI Visibility test module identifier used to correlate spans across worker processes.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_TEST_SESSION_ID
configurations.description: CI Visibility test session trace identifier used to correlate spans across worker processes.

configurations.name: DD_CODE_ORIGIN_FOR_SPANS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables code origin for spans behavior in the tracer. When disabled, related data collection and processing are skipped.

configurations.name: DD_CODE_ORIGIN_FOR_SPANS_EXPERIMENTAL_EXIT_SPANS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables code origin for spans experimental exit spans behavior in the tracer. When disabled, related data collection and processing are skipped.

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Crash Tracking. When enabled, the tracer initializes crash tracking and configures JVM crash/OOM hooks (for example, `-XX:OnError` and `-XX:OnOutOfMemoryError`) to run Datadog-provided scripts that upload `hs_err_pid*.log` crash logs and OOME notifications. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_CUSTOM_TRACE_ID
configurations.description: Custom variable set by the Jenkins Datadog Plugin for trace IDs.

configurations.name: DD_DATA_STREAMS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Data Streams Monitoring (DSM) and propagation. When enabled, the tracer registers the DSM propagator and reports pathway stats/payloads. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_DBM_PROPAGATION_MODE
configurations.description: Enables linking between data sent from APM and the Database Monitoring product when set to `service` or `full`. The `service` option enables the connection between DBM and APM services. The `full` option enables connection between database spans with database query events. Available for Postgres and MySQL.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_HOST
configurations.description: Override the address of the trace Agent host that the default tracer attempts to submit DogStatsD metrics to. Use `DD_AGENT_HOST` to override `DD_DOGSTATSD_HOST`.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT
configurations.description: The port of the DogStatsD Agent that metrics are submitted to. If the Agent configuration sets `dogstatsd_port` or `DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT` to something other than the default `8125`, then this tracing library `DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT` must match it.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_CAPTURE_TIMEOUT_MS
configurations.description: Timeout in milliseconds for capturing variable values during snapshot collection.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Dynamic Instrumentation.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_PROBE_FILE
configurations.description: Path to a local probe definition JSON file. When set, the tracer loads probe definitions from this file at startup instead of subscribing to remote configuration.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTED_IDENTIFIERS
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of additional identifier keywords to redact in captured snapshot data

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTION_EXCLUDED_IDENTIFIERS
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of identifier keywords to exclude from the default redaction list. Useful to prevent common keywords like password from being treated as sensitive for your use case.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_UPLOAD_INTERVAL_SECONDS
configurations.description: Interval in seconds between uploads of probe data.

configurations.name: DD_ENABLE_NX_SERVICE_NAME
configurations.description: Give DD_SERVICE precedence over NX_TASK_TARGET_PROJECT

configurations.name: DD_ENV
configurations.description: Sets the global `env` for all data emitted by the Agent. If `env` is not present in your trace data, this variable is used.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_APPSEC_STANDALONE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Disable apm tracing with legacy DD_EXPERIMENTAL_APPSEC_STANDALONE_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_FLAGGING_PROVIDER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the experimental feature-flag provider that evaluates flags from remote configuration. If not enabled, the provider is a no-op and returns default values.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_FLAGGING_PROVIDER_INITIALIZATION_TIMEOUT_MS
configurations.description: Timeout in milliseconds for OpenFeature provider initialization. If configuration is not received within this time, initialization fails. Can be configured via DD_EXPERIMENTAL_FLAGGING_PROVIDER_INITIALIZATION_TIMEOUT_MS environment variable.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_PROPAGATE_PROCESS_TAGS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables experimental propagation of process tags in payloads such as spans and telemetry.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_TEST_OPT_GIT_CACHE_DIR
configurations.description: Directory path used to persist the Test Optimization Git cache between runs.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_TEST_OPT_GIT_CACHE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables experimental test opt git cache behavior in the tracer. When disabled, related data collection and processing are skipped.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_TEST_OPT_SETTINGS_CACHE
configurations.description: Enables caching of Test Optimization settings to reduce repeated remote-configuration lookups between runs.

configurations.name: DD_EXTERNAL_ENV
configurations.description: Sets an external environment identifier attached to emitted telemetry for origin and environment attribution.

configurations.name: DD_FLAGGING_PROVIDER_ENABLED
configurations.description: To enable feature flagging capabilities in SDKs [PREVIEW]

configurations.name: DD_GIT_BRANCH
configurations.description: Overrides the Git branch value used by CI Visibility for repository and commit correlation.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_AUTHOR_DATE
configurations.description: The date when the author submitted the commit expressed in ISO 8601 format.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit author email reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_AUTHOR_NAME
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit author name reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_COMMITTER_DATE
configurations.description: The date when the committer submitted the commit expressed in ISO 8601 format.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit committer email reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_COMMITTER_NAME
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit committer name reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_HEAD_SHA
configurations.description: CI Visibility: sets the git head commit SHA for the current build/pull request, used to populate pull request info and CI git tags (for example, `git.commit.head.sha`) when user-supplied git metadata is needed.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_MESSAGE
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit message reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_SHA
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit SHA reported as CI metadata and used for git metadata tagging when enabled.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_FOLDER_PATH
configurations.description: Disables telemetry if inside a jest worker

configurations.name: DD_GIT_PROPERTIES_FILE
configurations.description: Disables telemetry if inside a jest worker

configurations.name: DD_GIT_PULL_REQUEST_BASE_BRANCH
configurations.description: CI Visibility: sets the pull request base/target branch name, used to populate pull request info and CI git tags (for example, `git.pull_request.base_branch`) when user-supplied git metadata is needed.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_PULL_REQUEST_BASE_BRANCH_SHA
configurations.description: Overrides the pull request base commit SHA reported as git metadata for CI features. Use this to set the base commit when it cannot be detected automatically.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_REPOSITORY_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the git repository URL reported as CI metadata and used for git metadata tagging when enabled. Any embedded credentials in the URL are stripped before use.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_TAG
configurations.description: Overrides the Git tag used by CI Visibility for repository and release correlation.

configurations.name: DD_GRPC_CLIENT_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: A range of errors can be accepted. By default, gRPC status codes 1 to 16 are reported as errors for gRPC clients. This configuration overrides that. Ex. `dd.grpc.client.error.statuses=1-4,7-10`

configurations.name: DD_GRPC_SERVER_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: A range of errors can be accepted. By default, gRPC status codes 2 to 16 are reported as errors for gRPC servers. This configuration overrides that. Ex. `dd.grpc.server.error.statuses=2-4,7-10`

configurations.name: DD_HEAP_SNAPSHOT_COUNT
configurations.description: Number of heap snapshots to write. Set to 0 to disable.

configurations.name: DD_HEAP_SNAPSHOT_DESTINATION
configurations.description: Directory path where heap snapshots are written.

configurations.name: DD_HEAP_SNAPSHOT_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Sets the delay between heap snapshots, affecting snapshot frequency and memory-profiling overhead.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_DB_ROWS_TO_TAINT
configurations.description: IAST: maximum number of JDBC `ResultSet` rows to treat as taint sources (SQL table data) for a given `ResultSet`. Rows beyond this limit are not tainted. Default: 1.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_DEDUPLICATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables vulnerability deduplication detection. When enabled, a vulnerability is only reported once in the lifetime of an app, instead of on every occurrence. Default value is true (enabled).

configurations.name: DD_IAST_ENABLED
configurations.description: IAST: controls IAST product activation. `true`/`1` fully enables IAST; `inactive` applies instrumentation but keeps logic inactive so it can be toggled via remote config; any other value fully disables IAST. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_MAX_CONCURRENT_REQUESTS
configurations.description: Maximum number of requests to be analyzed by IAST concurrently. Default value is 2.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_MAX_CONTEXT_OPERATIONS
configurations.description: Controls how many code vulnerabilities can be detected in the same request

configurations.name: DD_IAST_REDACTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables IAST redaction of sensitive data. Default value is in TracerSettings.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_REDACTION_NAME_PATTERN
configurations.description: Specifies a regex that will redact sensitive source names in vulnerability reports.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_REDACTION_VALUE_PATTERN
configurations.description: Specifies a regex that will redact sensitive source values in vulnerability reports.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_REQUEST_SAMPLING
configurations.description: Percentage of requests to be analyzed by IAST, between 1 and 100. Default value is 30.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_SECURITY_CONTROLS_CONFIGURATION
configurations.description: The Security Controls definition must be placed in the configuration variable `DD_IAST_SECURITY_CONTROLS_CONFIGURATION`.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_STACK_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables iast stack trace behavior in the tracer. When disabled, related data collection and processing are skipped.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_TELEMETRY_VERBOSITY
configurations.description: Specifies the verbosity of the sent telemetry. Default 'INFORMATION'

configurations.name: DD_INJECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables injection behavior in the tracer. When disabled, related data collection and processing are skipped.

configurations.name: DD_INJECT_FORCE
configurations.description: Single Step Instrumentation (SSI): forces the tracer javaagent to load even when multiple JVM agents are detected. This bypasses SSI guardrails that would otherwise abort startup to avoid running multiple agents. Can be set via `DD_INJECT_FORCE` or system property `dd.inject.force`. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_CONFIG_ID
configurations.description: Internal instrumentation configuration identifier attached to telemetry for remote-configuration correlation.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_ID
configurations.description: Sets the install ID included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers. This can be used to correlate telemetry to a specific installation.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_TIME
configurations.description: Sets the install time included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_TYPE
configurations.description: Sets the install type included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_TELEMETRY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Datadog may collect environmental and diagnostic information about your system to improve the product. When false, this telemetry data will not be collected. **Default**: true

configurations.name: DD_INTERNAL_PROFILING_LONG_LIVED_THRESHOLD
configurations.description: This is only used in testing to not have to wait 30s

configurations.name: DD_INTERNAL_PROFILING_TIMELINE_SAMPLING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables internal profiling timeline sampling behavior in the tracer. When disabled, related data collection and processing are skipped.

configurations.name: DD_LAMBDA_HANDLER
configurations.description: - Set the environment variable `DD_LAMBDA_HANDLER` to your original handler, for example, `myfunc.handler`.

configurations.name: DD_LANGCHAIN_SPAN_CHAR_LIMIT
configurations.description: Maximum number of characters recorded for langchain span data.

configurations.name: DD_LANGCHAIN_SPAN_PROMPT_COMPLETION_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Controls the sampling rate for capturing LangChain prompt and completion content on spans.

configurations.name: DD_LLMOBS_AGENTLESS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Set to `true` to disable sending data that requires a Datadog Agent.

configurations.name: DD_LLMOBS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Toggle to enable submitting data to LLM Observability

configurations.name: DD_LLMOBS_ML_APP
configurations.description: Sets the ML application name attached to LLM Observability spans and events.

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_INJECTION
configurations.description: Enable automatic injection of trace IDs in logs for supported logging libraries.

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_OTEL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables support for collecting and exporting logs generated by the the OpenTelemetry Logs API. This feature is available starting with .NET 3.1 when using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging

configurations.name: DD_LOG_LEVEL
configurations.description: Sets the internal log level for the tracer.

configurations.name: DD_METRICS_OTEL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables OpenTelemetry metrics export. Metrics are disabled by default and are only enabled when this is set to a truthy value.

configurations.name: DD_MINI_AGENT_PATH
configurations.description: Sets the filesystem path used to reach the mini-agent for tracer communication in constrained environments.

configurations.name: DD_OPENAI_LOGS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable collection of prompts and completions as logs. You can adjust the rate of prompts and completions collected using the sample rate configuration described below.

configurations.name: DD_OPENAI_SPAN_CHAR_LIMIT
configurations.description: Configure the maximum number of characters for the following data within span tags:

configurations.name: DD_PIPELINE_EXECUTION_ID
configurations.description: Sets the pipeline execution ID reported as CI metadata for AWS CodePipeline builds. This value is used as the CI pipeline identifier when running a CodeBuild job initiated by CodePipeline.

configurations.name: DD_PLAYWRIGHT_WORKER
configurations.description: Internal marker (set to `1`) used to detect Playwright worker processes for CI Visibility IPC payload routing.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ASYNC_CONTEXT_FRAME_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables profiling async context frame behavior in the tracer. When disabled, related data collection and processing are skipped.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_CODEHOTSPOTS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables code hotspots collection for the continuous profiler. When disabled, profiling data is not linked to tracing context for hotspot attribution.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_CPU_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXPERIMENTAL_OOM_HEAP_LIMIT_EXTENSION_SIZE
configurations.description: Sends a heap profile on OOM with async callback

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXPERIMENTAL_OOM_MONITORING_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXPORTERS
configurations.description: Code hotspots and endpoint tracing works

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_HEAP_ENABLED
configurations.description: If set to `true`, enables Live Heap profiling (in Preview). Defaults to `false`.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_HEAP_SAMPLING_INTERVAL

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_PPROF_PREFIX

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_PROFILERS

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_SOURCE_MAP

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_TIMELINE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable the timeline profile type. If both are set, this one takes precedence.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_UPLOAD_PERIOD

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT

configurations.name: DD_REMOTE_CONFIG_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS

configurations.name: DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_FLUSH_INTERVAL

configurations.name: DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_GC_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_RUNTIME_ID_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_SERVERLESS_PUBSUB_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE_MAPPING
configurations.description: **Example**: `mysql:my-mysql-service-name-db,pg:my-pg-service-name-db` Provide service names for each plugin. Accepts comma separated `plugin:service-name` pairs, with or without spaces.

configurations.name: DD_SITE
configurations.description: If you're not using the Datadog US1 site, set your Datadog site with a `DD_SITE` environment variable under the configuration tab of your function app, or copy the site parameter into the function code on line 21.

configurations.name: DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES
configurations.description: Span sampling rules to keep individual spans when the rest of the trace would otherwise be dropped. A JSON array of objects. Rules are applied in configured order to determine the span's sample rate. The sample_rate value must be between 0.0 and 1.0 (inclusive). For more information, see Ingestion Mechanisms. Example: - Set the span sample rate to 50% for the service my-service and operation name http.request, up to 50 traces per second: '[{"service": "my-service", "name": "http.request", "sample_rate":0.5, "max_per_second": 50}]'

configurations.name: DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES_FILE
configurations.description: Points to a JSON file that contains the span sampling rules. DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES takes precedence over this variable. See DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES for the rule format.

configurations.name: DD_TAGS

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_DEBUG

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_FORWARDER_PATH

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_LOG_COLLECTION_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_METRICS_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_TEST_FAILED_TEST_REPLAY_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_TEST_MANAGEMENT_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_128_BIT_TRACEID_GENERATION_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AGENT_PROTOCOL_VERSION
configurations.description: Protocol version to use for requests to the agent. The version configured must be supported by the agent version installed or all traces will be dropped.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CLIENT_IP_HEADER
configurations.description: Configures a custom header name from which to source the http.client_ip tag value. If this variable is set, all other IP-related headers are ignored (for example, setting DD_TRACE_CLIENT_IP_HEADER=custom-ip-header and including the header custom-ip-header: 5.6.7.9 in a request results in a span tagged with "http.client_ip": "5.6.7.9"). If an empty string or null value is passed, IP headers are queried in this order: - x-forwarded-for - x-real-ip - true-client-ip - x-client-ip - x-forwarded - forwarded-for - x-cluster-client-ip - fastly-client-ip - cf-connecting-ip - cf-connecting-ipv6

configurations.description: Enables instrumentation for vm. When disabled, spans for vm operations are not created.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing sent and received websocket messages (text and binary) and connection close events.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGES_INHERIT_SAMPLING
configurations.description: By default, websocket messages preserve the same sampling as the span captured during the handshake. This ensures that, if a handshake span has been sampled, all the messages in its session will also be sampled. To disable that behavior and sample each websocket message independently, set this configuration to `false`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGES_SEPARATE_TRACES
configurations.description: By default, each received message generates a new trace. The handshake is linked to it as a span link. Setting this parameter to `false` causes all the spans captured during the session to be in the same trace.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WHEN_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables instrumentation for when. When disabled, spans for when operations are not created.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WINSTON_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables instrumentation for winston. When disabled, spans for winston operations are not created.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WORKERPOOL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables instrumentation for workerpool. When disabled, spans for workerpool operations are not created.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables instrumentation for ws. When disabled, spans for ws operations are not created.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_X_DATADOG_TAGS_MAX_LENGTH
configurations.description: Maximum length of the `x-datadog-tags` header for trace tag propagation. Set to 0 to disable.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE__CONFLUENTINC_KAFKA_JAVASCRIPT_ENABLED
configurations.description: null

configurations.name: DD_TRACING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing behavior in the tracer. When disabled, related data collection and processing are skipped.

configurations.name: DD_VERSION
configurations.description: Set the application's version, for example: `1.2.3` or `6c44da20`.

configurations.name: DD_VERTEXAI_SPAN_CHAR_LIMIT
configurations.description: Maximum number of characters recorded for vertexai span data.

configurations.name: DD_VERTEXAI_SPAN_PROMPT_COMPLETION_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Controls the sampling rate for capturing Vertex AI prompt and completion content on spans.

configurations.name: DD_VITEST_WORKER
configurations.description: Internal marker (set to `1`) used to detect Vitest worker processes for CI Visibility IPC payload routing.

configurations.name: OTEL_BSP_MAX_EXPORT_BATCH_SIZE
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of items exported per OpenTelemetry batch processor flush.

configurations.name: OTEL_BSP_MAX_QUEUE_SIZE
configurations.description: Maximum logs to queue before dropping

configurations.name: OTEL_BSP_SCHEDULE_DELAY
configurations.description: Sets the delay between OpenTelemetry batch processor export cycles, in milliseconds.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: Specifies the base URL for sending OTLP data for all signals unless overridden. **Default (gRPC)**: `http://localhost:4317`. **Default (HTTP)**: `http://localhost:4318`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS
configurations.description: Configuration key to set custom headers for OTLP export (fallback for metrics-specific headers). Used when metrics-specific OTLP headers are not set. Format: api-key=key,other=value.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: Specifies the URL for sending OTLP logs. Takes precedence over `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`. : **Default (gRPC)**: `http://localhost:4317` : **Default (HTTP)**: `http://localhost:4318/v1/logs`

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_HEADERS
configurations.description: Specifies a comma-separated list of key-value pairs to be used as headers on outgoing OTLP logs requests. Takes precedence over the general `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Specifies the OTLP transport protocol for logs. Takes precedence over `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL`. : **Accepted values**: `grpc`, `http/protobuf`, `http/json`
configurations.default_value: SDK-dependent, but will typically be either http/protobuf or grpc.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Specifies the timeout (in milliseconds) for a single outgoing OTLP logs request. Takes precedence over the general `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: If non-empty, used as the OTLP metrics endpoint. Otherwise falls back to `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`, then to `http://<DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL host>:4317/4318` based on protocol. For `http/protobuf`, `/v1/metrics` is appended if missing.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_HEADERS
configurations.description: Specifies a comma-separated list of key-value pairs to be used as headers on outgoing OTLP metrics requests (for example, `api-key=key,other-config=value`). Takes precedence over the general `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Specifies the OTLP transport protocol to use for metrics data. Takes precedence over the general `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL`. **Accepted values**: `grpc`, `http/protobuf`, `http/json`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TEMPORALITY_PREFERENCE
configurations.description: Controls metrics aggregation temporality. Accepted values: `delta` or `cumulative` (case-insensitive); empty/invalid values are treated as `delta`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Used as the exporter network timeout (ms) for OTLP metrics requests. If set to `0`, the library falls back to `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Specifies the transport protocol to use for all signals unless overridden. **Accepted values**: `grpc`, `http/protobuf`, `http/json`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the request timeout for OTLP export (fallback for metrics-specific timeout). Used when the metrics-specific OTLP timeout is not set. Default value is 10000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER
configurations.description: Controls the OpenTelemetry logs exporter selection. This setting is not supported and is ignored.

configurations.name: OTEL_LOG_LEVEL
configurations.description: Enables debug mode, producing verbose tracer logs (not recommended for production or sensitive environments) when set to debug. Alias for DD_TRACE_DEBUG.

configurations.name: OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER
configurations.description: Specifies the metrics exporter to be used. **Notes**: The only accepted values are `otlp` and `none`. A value of `none` disables the emission of OTel metrics, as well as APM runtime metrics (equivalent to `DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_ENABLED=false`)

configurations.name: OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_INTERVAL
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the time interval (in milliseconds) between metric export attempts. **Default**: 10000 (10s) **Notes**: This default value is Datadog's recommended configuration and differs from the OpenTelemetry specification's default of 60000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the maximum allowed time (in milliseconds) to collect and export metrics. **Default**: 7500 (7.5s) **Notes**: This default value is Datadog's recommended configuration and differs from the OpenTelemetry specification's default of 30000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_PROPAGATORS
configurations.description: Configures which propagators are used for extracting and injecting trace context.

configurations.name: OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES
configurations.description: Set OpenTelemetry resource attributes as a list of `(key, value)` pairs.

configurations.name: OTEL_SDK_DISABLED
configurations.description: Disables the Datadog SDK's OpenTelemetry interoperability for all signals. **Notes**: When set to `true`, this effectively sets `DD_TRACE_OTEL_ENABLED=false`, `DD_LOGS_OTEL_ENABLED=false`, and `DD_METRICS_OTEL_ENABLED=false`. **Ruby & Go SDKs**: The OpenTelemetry SDK activates automatically upon import and configuration, so this setting is not applicable.

configurations.name: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
configurations.description: Your application's default service name, set as the service tag on traces. Alias for DD_SERVICE.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER
configurations.description: Enables or disables tracing. When disabled, instrumentation still runs but no trace data is sent. Alias for DD_TRACE_ENABLED.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER
configurations.description: Sets the trace sampling rate, controlling the fraction of traces kept. Alias for DD_TRACE_SAMPLE_RATE.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG
configurations.description: Sets the argument for OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER when using the traceidratio sampler (for example, OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG=0.25). This is used to derive the tracer sample rate when mapping OpenTelemetry configuration.
